content.problemStatement: Write a program for the coding challenge "Pipeline". Refer to LeetCode description: https://codeforces.com/contest/287/problem/B
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer n, which is the number of segments. Then n lines follow, each containing two integers ai and bi, which are the start and end points of the segment.
content.outputFormat: The minimum number of points on the number line that are needed to cover all segments.

hidden.label: tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed

solution.approach: Sort the segments by their end points and then use a greedy approach to select the minimum number of points.

code.Python3:
def solve(segments):
    segments.sort(key=lambda x: x[1])
    points = 1
    last = segments[0][1]
    for segment in segments[1:]:
        if segment[0] > last:
            points += 1
            last = segment[1]
    return points

data = int(input())
segments = [tuple(map(int, input().split())) for _ in range(data)]
print(solve(segments))
